title: Question: which combinations are possible: call podman from docker, docker from podman, etc.
body:

You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

comments.body: @PavelSosin-320 could you make your question clearer? At this point, the question is too wide to provide an accurate response.

Docker and Podman are two different container engines.  They work independently and neither requires the other.  Both work with the same container images and container registries and both launch containers using the same tools to configure the linux kernel to run containers (runc).

If Docker-In-Docker image exists and used as Dockerized Docker build environment is it possible to create Docker-in-Podman and Podman-in-Docker to use as dockerized build environment.? 
Technically it means is it possible to run Docker engine inside Podman container and vise-versa?
It should be possible to work this way yes.
